{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_017ebsc6Jg4bpTi7VBhiWJB5","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01SxpucrTV7CpsoLBHAY72it","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/work/parse_and_simulate.py","content":"\"\"\"Parse raw supply chain documents and reconstruct batch flows.\n\nOutputs:\n  work/sold_composition.csv : per (day, store, product) which producer's units were sold\n  work/delivery_check.txt   : validation of TOTAL_DELIVERED_UNITS\n\"\"\"\nimport os, re, json, csv\nfrom collections import defaultdict, deque\n\nBASE = '/app'\n\n# ---------- 1. Parse store orders ----------\n# orders[(day, store)] = {product: qty}\norders = defaultdict(dict)\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_orders'):\n    m = re.match(r'day_(\\d+)_(S_\\d+)_(.*)\\.(txt|csv|json)$', fn)\n    day, store, fmt = int(m.group(1)), m.group(2), m.group(3)\n    path = f'{BASE}/raw_orders/{fn}'\n    if fmt == 'api-json':\n        d = json.load(open(path))\n        assert d['store_id'] == store and int(d['date'].split('_')[1]) == day\n        for it in d['items']:\n            orders[(day, store)][it['prod_id']] = int(it['qty'])\n    elif fmt == 'table-csv':\n        for row in csv.DictReader(open(path), delimiter=';'):\n            orders[(day, store)][row['PRODUCT']] = int(row['QUANTITY'])\n    elif fmt == 'key-value':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'ORDER_PRODUCT: (P_\\d+) \\| ORDER_QTY: (\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                orders[(day, store)][mm.group(1)] = int(mm.group(2))\n    elif fmt == 'text-template':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'deliver (\\d+) units of product (P_\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                orders[(day, store)][mm.group(2)] = int(mm.group(1))\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(fmt)\n\n# ---------- 2. Parse supplier receipts ----------\n# supply[(day, supplier)] = {product: qty}\nsupply = defaultdict(dict)\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_supplier_receipts'):\n    m = re.match(r'day_(\\d+)_(PR_\\d+)_(.*)\\.(txt|csv|json)$', fn)\n    day, sup, fmt = int(m.group(1)), m.group(2), m.group(3)\n    path = f'{BASE}/raw_supplier_receipts/{fn}'\n    if fmt == 'api-json':\n        d = json.load(open(path))\n        assert d['supplier_id'] == sup\n        for it in d['items']:\n            supply[(day, sup)][it['prod_id']] = int(it['delivered_qty'])\n    elif fmt == 'table-csv':\n        for row in csv.DictReader(open(path), delimiter=';'):\n            supply[(day, sup)][row['PRODUCT']] = int(row['DELIVERED_QTY'])\n    elif fmt == 'key-value':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'PRODUCT: (P_\\d+) \\| DELIVERED_QTY: (\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                supply[(day, sup)][mm.group(1)] = int(mm.group(2))\n    elif fmt == 'text-template':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'delivered (\\d+) units of product (P_\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                supply[(day, sup)][mm.group(2)] = int(mm.group(1))\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(fmt)\n\n# ---------- 3. Parse wholesaler receipts (supplier acceptance order) ----------\n# sup_order[day] = [supplier ids sorted by seq]\nsup_seq = defaultdict(list)\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_receipts'):\n    txt = open(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_receipts/{fn}').read()\n    day = int(re.search(r'DAY: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    sup = re.search(r'SUPPLIER_ID: (PR_\\d+)', txt).group(1)\n    seq = int(re.search(r'DAILY_SEQUENCE_NO: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    sup_seq[day].append((seq, sup))\nsup_order = {d: [s for _, s in sorted(v)] for d, v in sup_seq.items()}\n\n# ---------- 4. Parse wholesaler->store receipts ----------\nstore_seq = defaultdict(list)\nstore_total = {}\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_store_receipts'):\n    txt = open(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_store_receipts/{fn}').read()\n    day = int(re.search(r'DAY: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    st = re.search(r'STORE_ID: (S_\\d+)', txt).group(1)\n    seq = int(re.search(r'DAILY_SEQUENCE_NO: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    tot = int(re.search(r'TOTAL_DELIVERED_UNITS: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    store_seq[day].append((seq, st))\n    store_total[(day, st)] = tot\nstore_order = {d: [s for _, s in sorted(v)] for d, v in store_seq.items()}\n\nDAYS = sorted(sup_order)\nprint('days:', DAYS[0], DAYS[-1], len(DAYS))\n\n# ---------- 5. Simulate wholesaler FIFO ----------\n# queue per product: deque of [qty, producer, supply_day]\nwq = defaultdict(deque)\n# deliveries[(day, store)] = {product: [(qty, producer, supply_day), ...]} in FIFO order\ndeliveries = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))\nmismatch = 0\nreport = []\nfor day in DAYS:\n    for sup in sup_order[day]:\n        for prod, qty in supply.get((day, sup), {}).items():\n            wq[prod].append([qty, sup, day])\n    for st in store_order[day]:\n        delivered_total = 0\n        for prod, qty in orders.get((day, st), {}).items():\n            need = qty\n            q = wq[prod]\n            while need > 0 and q:\n                frag = q[0]\n                take = min(need, frag[0])\n                deliveries[(day, st)][prod].append((take, frag[1], frag[2]))\n                frag[0] -= take\n                need -= take\n                delivered_total += take\n                if frag[0] == 0:\n                    q.popleft()\n        expected = store_total[(day, st)]\n        if delivered_total != expected:\n            mismatch += 1\n            report.append(f'MISMATCH day {day} {st}: sim {delivered_total} vs receipt {expected}')\nprint('store-day delivery mismatches:', mismatch)\nwith open(f'{BASE}/work/delivery_check.txt', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(f'mismatches: {mismatch}\\n')\n    f.write('\\n'.join(report[:50]))\n\n# ---------- 6. Store-side FIFO + sales consumption ----------\nimport pandas as pd\nsales = pd.read_csv(f'{BASE}/sales_log.csv')\nsold = sales.groupby(['Day', 'Store_ID', 'Product_ID'], sort=False)['Sold_Quantity'].sum()\nsold_d = sold.to_dict()\n\n# store queues per (store, product)\nsq = defaultdict(deque)\nrows = []  # day, store, product, producer, supply_day, qty_sold\nerrors = 0\nSTORES = sorted({s for _, s in store_total}, key=lambda x: int(x.split('_')[1]))\nPRODUCTS = [f'P_{i}' for i in range(1, 31)]\nfor day in DAYS:\n    # sales happen first (units delivered day d sellable from d+1) -- to verify\n    for st in STORES:\n        for prod in PRODUCTS:\n            need = sold_d.get((day, st, prod), 0)\n            q = sq[(st, prod)]\n            while need > 0 and q:\n                frag = q[0]\n                take = min(need, frag[0])\n                rows.append((day, st, prod, frag[1], frag[2], take))\n                frag[0] -= take\n                need -= take\n                if frag[0] == 0:\n                    q.popleft()\n            if need > 0:\n                errors += 1\n    # then deliveries arrive\n    for st in STORES:\n        dd = deliveries.get((day, st))\n        if dd:\n            for prod, frags in dd.items():\n                for qty, producer, sday in frags:\n                    sq[(st, prod)].append([qty, producer, sday])\nprint('store sale shortfall errors (sales-before-delivery convention):', errors)\n\ndf = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['Day', 'Store_ID', 'Product_ID', 'Producer', 'Supply_Day', 'Qty'])\ndf.to_csv(f'{BASE}/work/sold_composition.csv', index=False)\nprint('sold composition rows:', len(df))\nprint(df.head(20))\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":758,"cache_read_input_tokens":39512,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":758,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":46,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"280af500-454c-4601-925d-666e53b1cc05","request_id":"req_011CbwN3dWQLNRgz28TQUm8D"}

Parse store orders ----------\n# orders[(day, store)] = {product: qty}\norders = defaultdict(dict)\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_orders'):\n    m = re.match(r'day_(\\d+)_(S_\\d+)_(.*)\\.(txt|csv|json)$', fn)\n    day, store, fmt = int(m.group(1)), m.group(2), m.group(3)\n    path = f'{BASE}/raw_orders/{fn}'\n    if fmt == 'api-json':\n        d = json.load(open(path))\n        assert d['store_id'] == store and int(d['date'].split('_')[1]) == day\n        for it in d['items']:\n            orders[(day, store)][it['prod_id']] = int(it['qty'])\n    elif fmt == 'table-csv':\n        for row in csv.DictReader(open(path), delimiter=';'):\n            orders[(day, store)][row['PRODUCT']] = int(row['QUANTITY'])\n    elif fmt == 'key-value':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'ORDER_PRODUCT: (P_\\d+) \\| ORDER_QTY: (\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                orders[(day, store)][mm.group(1)] = int(mm.group(2))\n    elif fmt == 'text-template':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'deliver (\\d+) units of product (P_\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                orders[(day, store)][mm.group(2)] = int(mm.group(1))\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(fmt)\n\n# ---------- 2. Parse supplier receipts ----------\n# supply[(day, supplier)] = {product: qty}\nsupply = defaultdict(dict)\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_supplier_receipts'):\n    m = re.match(r'day_(\\d+)_(PR_\\d+)_(.*)\\.(txt|csv|json)$', fn)\n    day, sup, fmt = int(m.group(1)), m.group(2), m.group(3)\n    path = f'{BASE}/raw_supplier_receipts/{fn}'\n    if fmt == 'api-json':\n        d = json.load(open(path))\n        assert d['supplier_id'] == sup\n        for it in d['items']:\n            supply[(day, sup)][it['prod_id']] = int(it['delivered_qty'])\n    elif fmt == 'table-csv':\n        for row in csv.DictReader(open(path), delimiter=';'):\n            supply[(day, sup)][row['PRODUCT']] = int(row['DELIVERED_QTY'])\n    elif fmt == 'key-value':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'PRODUCT: (P_\\d+) \\| DELIVERED_QTY: (\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                supply[(day, sup)][mm.group(1)] = int(mm.group(2))\n    elif fmt == 'text-template':\n        for line in open(path):\n            mm = re.search(r'delivered (\\d+) units of product (P_\\d+)', line)\n            if mm:\n                supply[(day, sup)][mm.group(2)] = int(mm.group(1))\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(fmt)\n\n# ---------- 3. Parse wholesaler receipts (supplier acceptance order) ----------\n# sup_order[day] = [supplier ids sorted by seq]\nsup_seq = defaultdict(list)\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_receipts'):\n    txt = open(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_receipts/{fn}').read()\n    day = int(re.search(r'DAY: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    sup = re.search(r'SUPPLIER_ID: (PR_\\d+)', txt).group(1)\n    seq = int(re.search(r'DAILY_SEQUENCE_NO: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    sup_seq[day].append((seq, sup))\nsup_order = {d: [s for _, s in sorted(v)] for d, v in sup_seq.items()}\n\n# ---------- 4. Parse wholesaler->store receipts ----------\nstore_seq = defaultdict(list)\nstore_total = {}\nfor fn in os.listdir(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_store_receipts'):\n    txt = open(f'{BASE}/raw_wholesaler_store_receipts/{fn}').read()\n    day = int(re.search(r'DAY: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    st = re.search(r'STORE_ID: (S_\\d+)', txt).group(1)\n    seq = int(re.search(r'DAILY_SEQUENCE_NO: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    tot = int(re.search(r'TOTAL_DELIVERED_UNITS: (\\d+)', txt).group(1))\n    store_seq[day].append((seq, st))\n    store_total[(day, st)] = tot\nstore_order = {d: [s for _, s in sorted(v)] for d, v in store_seq.items()}\n\nDAYS = sorted(sup_order)\nprint('days:', DAYS[0], DAYS[-1], len(DAYS))\n\n# ---------- 5. Simulate wholesaler FIFO ----------\n# queue per product: deque of [qty, producer, supply_day]\nwq = defaultdict(deque)\n# deliveries[(day, store)] = {product: [(qty, producer, supply_day), ...]} in FIFO order\ndeliveries = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))\nmismatch = 0\nreport = []\nfor day in DAYS:\n    for sup in sup_order[day]:\n        for prod, qty in supply.get((day, sup), {}).items():\n            wq[prod].append([qty, sup, day])\n    for st in store_order[day]:\n        delivered_total = 0\n        for prod, qty in orders.get((day, st), {}).items():\n            need = qty\n            q = wq[prod]\n            while need > 0 and q:\n                frag = q[0]\n                take = min(need, frag[0])\n                deliveries[(day, st)][prod].append((take, frag[1], frag[2]))\n                frag[0] -= take\n                need -= take\n                delivered_total += take\n                if frag[0] == 0:\n                    q.popleft()\n        expected = store_total[(day, st)]\n        if delivered_total != expected:\n            mismatch += 1\n            report.append(f'MISMATCH day {day} {st}: sim {delivered_total} vs receipt {expected}')\nprint('store-day delivery mismatches:', mismatch)\nwith open(f'{BASE}/work/delivery_check.txt', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(f'mismatches: {mismatch}\\n')\n    f.write('\\n'.join(report[:50]))\n\n# ---------- 6. Store-side FIFO + sales consumption ----------\nimport pandas as pd\nsales = pd.read_csv(f'{BASE}/sales_log.csv')\nsold = sales.groupby(['Day', 'Store_ID', 'Product_ID'], sort=False)['Sold_Quantity'].sum()\nsold_d = sold.to_dict()\n\n# store queues per (store, product)\nsq = defaultdict(deque)\nrows = []  # day, store, product, producer, supply_day, qty_sold\nerrors = 0\nSTORES = sorted({s for _, s in store_total}, key=lambda x: int(x.split('_')[1]))\nPRODUCTS = [f'P_{i}' for i in range(1, 31)]\nfor day in DAYS:\n    # sales happen first (units delivered day d sellable from d+1) -- to verify\n    for st in STORES:\n        for prod in PRODUCTS:\n            need = sold_d.get((day, st, prod), 0)\n            q = sq[(st, prod)]\n            while need > 0 and q:\n                frag = q[0]\n                take = min(need, frag[0])\n                rows.append((day, st, prod, frag[1], frag[2], take))\n                frag[0] -= take\n                need -= take\n                if frag[0] == 0:\n                    q.popleft()\n            if need > 0:\n                errors += 1\n    # then deliveries arrive\n    for st in STORES:\n        dd = deliveries.get((day, st))\n        if dd:\n            for prod, frags in dd.items():\n                for qty, producer, sday in frags:\n                    sq[(st, prod)].append([qty, producer, sday])\nprint('store sale shortfall errors (sales-before-delivery convention):', errors)\n\ndf = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['Day', 'Store_ID', 'Product_ID', 'Producer', 'Supply_Day', 'Qty'])\ndf.to_csv(f'{BASE}/work/sold_composition.csv', index=False)\nprint('sold composition rows:', len(df))\nprint(df.head(20))\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01GYpC4U7zm2Kms9gVTgoDda","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01CWRnDEaSg29mdi1KNDccsc","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nresid = {}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = (D[col] / cal[col[1]])\n    lev = base.median()\n    resid[col] = base/lev - 1.0   # relative residual\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\nsig = R.std()\nprint('typical residual sigma:', sig.median())\n\n# find onsets: day t with z<-3, previous day z>-1.5\nevents=[]\nfor col in R.columns:\n    z = R[col]/sig[col]\n    for t in range(2, 364):\n        d=t+1  # index alignment: R.index is Day\n        if z.loc[d] < -3 and z.loc[d-1] > -1.5:\n            events.append((col, d, z.loc[d]))\nprint('n events:', len(events))\n# average trajectory aligned at onset\nW=[]\nfor col, d, _ in events:\n    if d>5 and d<345:\n        w = R[col].loc[d-4:d+18].values\n        W.append(w/( -R[col].loc[d] ))  # normalize by dip depth\nW=np.array(W)\nprint('aligned mean trajectory (day -4..+18, normalized to dip=-1):')\nfor i,v in enumerate(W.mean(axis=0)):\n    print(i-4, round(v,3))\nEOF","description":"Align dip events and extract empirical recovery kernel"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4891,"cache_read_input_tokens":56429,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4891,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":73,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"e1106395-72c1-4832-9ff1-b57ab5a2ac75","request_id":"req_011CbwNPKmrkWJvhjaaVuNZE"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_018T4fPZxd1RASpbP2qXSCkL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01KJhvFN5JuiEGxYMTG6whjN","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\n\n# residual matrix (relative)\nresid = {}\nlev = {}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col] / cal[col[1]]\n    l = base.median(); lev[col]=l\n    resid[col] = base/l - 1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\n\nPRODUCERS = sorted(sc.Producer.unique(), key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1]))\nprods = [f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]\nstores = sorted({c[0] for c in D.columns}, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\n\nresults = {}\nfor p in prods:\n    rows_X, rows_y = [], []\n    sub = sc[sc.Product_ID==p]\n    piv = sub.pivot_table(index=['Store_ID','Day'], columns='Producer', values='Qty', aggfunc='sum').fillna(0)\n    for s in stores:\n        if s not in piv.index.get_level_values(0): continue\n        sp = piv.loc[s]\n        for d in sp.index:\n            if d+1 > 365: continue\n            y = R[(s,p)].loc[d+1]\n            if not np.isfinite(y): continue\n            x = sp.loc[d] / lev[(s,p)]   # units sold as fraction of typical daily demand\n            rows_X.append(x); rows_y.append(y)\n    X = pd.DataFrame(rows_X).fillna(0); y = np.array(rows_y)\n    cols = X.columns\n    Xv = X.values\n    XtX = Xv.T@Xv + 1e-6*np.eye(Xv.shape[1])\n    beta = np.linalg.solve(XtX, Xv.T@y)\n    se = np.sqrt(np.diag(np.linalg.inv(XtX)) * np.var(y - Xv@beta))\n    t = beta/se\n    res = pd.Series(t, index=cols).sort_values()\n    results[p] = res\n    sig = res[res < -4]\n    print(p, 'strong negative producers:', {k: round(v,1) for k,v in sig.items()})\nimport pickle\npickle.dump(results, open('/app/work/reg_results.pkl','wb'))\nEOF","description":"Regress next-day residual on producer sale quantities per product","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4982,"cache_read_input_tokens":61320,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4982,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":62,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"5d401537-e961-4126-975c-50bed68796eb","request_id":"req_011CbwNVFqtbxcD4w3AfsjR4"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01HcUGyf5P9KTgDvJKYYctV2","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01F3VQjMBxqHLtMBkwyLZqxN","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\nresid={}; lev={}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col]/cal[col[1]]; l=base.median(); lev[col]=l; resid[col]=base/l-1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\n\ndef study(p, pr):\n    sub = sc[sc.Product_ID==p]\n    g = sub.groupby(['Store_ID','Day'])\n    qty = g.apply(lambda x: x[x.Producer==pr].Qty.sum(), include_groups=False)\n    tot = g.Qty.sum()\n    nprod = g.Producer.nunique()\n    sole = qty[(qty>0)&(nprod==1)]\n    print(f'{p} {pr}: days sold any={len(qty[qty>0])}, sole-producer days={len(sole)}')\n    trajs=[]\n    for (s,d), q in sole.items():\n        if d>360: continue\n        f = q/lev[(s,p)]\n        w = R[(s,p)].loc[d-2:d+10].values\n        if len(w)==13 and np.isfinite(w).all():\n            trajs.append((w/f, f, s, d, q))\n    if trajs:\n        W = np.array([t[0] for t in trajs])\n        print(' mean traj/f (day -2..+10):', np.round(W.mean(axis=0),2))\n        print(' n traj:', len(W), ' median f:', np.median([t[1] for t in trajs]).round(2))\n        # show a few raw examples\n        for t in trajs[:4]:\n            print('  ex:', t[2], 'day', t[3], 'q', t[4], 'f=%.2f'%t[1], np.round(t[0]*t[1],2))\nstudy('P_13','PR_3')\nstudy('P_10','PR_58')\nstudy('P_24','PR_38')\nstudy('P_8','PR_28')\nEOF","description":"Examine sole-producer sale days for top suspect pairs","timeout":180000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4514,"cache_read_input_tokens":66302,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4514,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"cd9a449e-f5b6-48d4-86c1-55244ba0791a","request_id":"req_011CbwNaBBytTp7FyaVT5Wed"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01PmVJnjP4Xs3cYRf1qyVyRB","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Ckij2qxAwemU1KUJqiVLB3","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\nreg = pickle.load(open('/app/work/reg_results.pkl','rb'))\nresid={}; lev={}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col]/cal[col[1]]; l=base.median(); lev[col]=l; resid[col]=base/l-1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\n\nflagged = {p: list(res[res<-4].index) for p,res in reg.items()}\n# pooled lag regression: r_{d+k} ~ f_d  (f = flagged producer units / level), per lag k\nlags = range(-3, 17)\nnum = {k:0.0 for k in lags}; den = {k:0.0 for k in lags}\nfor p, prs in flagged.items():\n    sub = sc[(sc.Product_ID==p)&(sc.Producer.isin(prs))]\n    g = sub.groupby(['Store_ID','Day']).Qty.sum()\n    for (s,d), q in g.items():\n        f = q/lev[(s,p)]\n        for k in lags:\n            dd = d+k\n            if 1<=dd<=365:\n                r = R[(s,p)].loc[dd]\n                if np.isfinite(r):\n                    num[k] += f*r; den[k] += f*f\nprint('lag profile beta_k (r_{d+k} ~ f_d):')\nfor k in lags:\n    print(k, round(num[k]/den[k],4))\nEOF","description":"Pooled lag regression to extract empirical recovery kernel","timeout":180000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4259,"cache_read_input_tokens":70816,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4259,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":39,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"788a64a1-37cd-4188-82d7-ec8bfa76959a","request_id":"req_011CbwNdrAMkYKTQZC5Azo4E"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01PJQcjGRszvkHiCGixswmYo","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01BWCWcpAne8WF43dGU1NWmM","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\nresid={}; lev={}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col]/cal[col[1]]; l=base.median(); lev[col]=l; resid[col]=base/l-1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\nk = np.array([1.0, 1.04, 1.01, 0.51, 0.45, 0.37, 0.33, 0.07,0.07,0.07,0.07,0.07,0.07,0.07])\n\nprods=[f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]\nstores = sorted({c[0] for c in D.columns}, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nPRODUCERS = sorted(sc.Producer.unique(), key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1]))\npidx = {pr:i for i,pr in enumerate(PRODUCERS)}\n\nout = {}\nfor p in prods:\n    sub = sc[sc.Product_ID==p]\n    X_rows=[]; y_rows=[]\n    for s in stores:\n        ss = sub[sub.Store_ID==s]\n        U = np.zeros((366, 60))\n        for r_ in ss.itertuples():\n            U[r_.Day, pidx[r_.Producer]] += r_.Qty\n        U /= lev[(s,p)]\n        # convolve with kernel: G[d] = sum_k k[k]*U[d-k]\n        G = np.zeros((366,60))\n        for kk,kv in enumerate(k, start=1):\n            G[1+kk:366,:] += kv*U[1:366-kk,:]\n        rvec = R[(s,p)].reindex(range(366)).values\n        mask = np.isfinite(rvec) & (np.arange(366)>=2)\n        X_rows.append(G[mask]); y_rows.append(rvec[mask])\n    X = np.vstack(X_rows); y = np.concatenate(y_rows)\n    keep = X.sum(axis=0) > 0\n    Xk = X[:, keep]\n    XtX = Xk.T@Xk + 1e-8*np.eye(Xk.shape[1])\n    beta = np.linalg.solve(XtX, Xk.T@y)\n    res = y - Xk@beta\n    sigma2 = (res**2).sum()/(len(y)-Xk.shape[1])\n    se = np.sqrt(np.diag(np.linalg.inv(XtX))*sigma2)\n    t = beta/se\n    names = [PRODUCERS[i] for i in range(60) if keep[i]]\n    ts = pd.Series(t, index=names)\n    bs = pd.Series(beta, index=names)\n    out[p] = pd.DataFrame({'beta':bs, 't':ts}).sort_values('t')\npickle.dump(out, open('/app/work/kernel_reg.pkl','wb'))\nallt = np.concatenate([df.t.values for df in out.values()])\nprint('t percentiles:', np.percentile(allt,[0.5,1,5,25,50,75,95,99,99.5]))\nprint('count t<-4:', (allt<-4).sum(), ' t<-5:', (allt<-5).sum(), ' t<-6:', (allt<-6).sum(), 't>4:', (allt>4).sum(), 't>3:',(allt>3).sum())\nfor p in prods:\n    df = out[p]\n    sig = df[df.t<-4]\n    print(p, [(i, round(r.beta,3), round(r.t,1)) for i,r in sig.iterrows()])\nEOF","description":"Kernel-convolved regression per product over all producers","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1498,"cache_read_input_tokens":87296,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1498,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":75,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"a94dce26-db76-4f35-987c-ec407355badc","request_id":"req_011CbwNwhELkY1TU7WJ1Djaf"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01HU1pHfT7z38M2tPxU2HNGW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01JKmjNGmLT1iVAaZxiwkLDx","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\nout = pickle.load(open('/app/work/kernel_reg.pkl','rb'))\nresid={}; lev={}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col]/cal[col[1]]; l=base.median(); lev[col]=l; resid[col]=base/l-1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\nk = np.array([1.0, 1.04, 1.01, 0.51, 0.45, 0.37, 0.33]+[0.07]*7)\nprods=[f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]\nstores = sorted({c[0] for c in D.columns}, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nPRODUCERS = sorted(sc.Producer.unique(), key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1]))\n\n# build split design: for candidate pairs, two columns (even/odd supply day); others single\nres_rows=[]\nfor p in prods:\n    df = out[p]\n    cands = list(df[df.t < -3].index)\n    sub = sc[sc.Product_ID==p]\n    cols = []  # list of (producer, half or None)\n    for pr in sorted(sub.Producer.unique(), key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1])):\n        if pr in cands:\n            cols.append((pr,0)); cols.append((pr,1))\n        else:\n            cols.append((pr,None))\n    cidx = {c:i for i,c in enumerate(cols)}\n    X_rows=[]; y_rows=[]\n    for s in stores:\n        ss = sub[sub.Store_ID==s]\n        U = np.zeros((366, len(cols)))\n        for r_ in ss.itertuples():\n            key = (r_.Producer, None)\n            if key not in cidx:\n                key = (r_.Producer, r_.Supply_Day % 2)\n            U[r_.Day, cidx[key]] += r_.Qty\n        U /= lev[(s,p)]\n        G = np.zeros((366,len(cols)))\n        for kk,kv in enumerate(k, start=1):\n            G[1+kk:366,:] += kv*U[1:366-kk,:]\n        rvec = R[(s,p)].reindex(range(366)).values\n        mask = np.isfinite(rvec) & (np.arange(366)>=2)\n        X_rows.append(G[mask]); y_rows.append(rvec[mask])\n    X = np.vstack(X_rows); y = np.concatenate(y_rows)\n    keep = X.sum(axis=0)>0\n    Xk = X[:,keep]; names=[c for c,kc in zip(cols,keep) if kc]\n    XtX = Xk.T@Xk + 1e-8*np.eye(Xk.shape[1])\n    beta = np.linalg.solve(XtX, Xk.T@y)\n    sigma2 = ((y-Xk@beta)**2).sum()/(len(y)-Xk.shape[1])\n    se = np.sqrt(np.diag(np.linalg.inv(XtX))*sigma2)\n    t = beta/se\n    d = {nm:(b,tt) for nm,b,tt in zip(names,beta,t)}\n    for pr in cands:\n        b0,t0 = d.get((pr,0),(np.nan,np.nan)); b1,t1 = d.get((pr,1),(np.nan,np.nan))\n        full = out[p].loc[pr]\n        res_rows.append((p, pr, round(full.beta,3), round(full.t,1), round(b0,3), round(t0,1), round(b1,3), round(t1,1)))\nrr = pd.DataFrame(res_rows, columns=['product','producer','beta','t','b_even','t_even','b_odd','t_odd'])\nrr.to_csv('/app/work/split_half.csv', index=False)\npd.set_option('display.max_rows',200)\nprint(rr.sort_values('t').to_string())\nEOF","description":"Split-half replication test for candidate pairs","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7737,"cache_read_input_tokens":88794,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7737,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":53,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"dd6c2d9a-7776-4875-bd4a-2c902b27e3db","request_id":"req_011CbwP3QWRjZ6HM9F1pbVRe"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01Uz9r1bzeY97fmuybR2DT98","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01EyaC8w1tgrupSjseEYex1t","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\nresid={}; lev={}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col]/cal[col[1]]; l=base.median(); lev[col]=l; resid[col]=base/l-1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\ng = np.array([1.0, 1.04, 1.01, 0.51, 0.45, 0.37, 0.33])\ngg = (g*g).sum()\n\ndef batch_effects(p, pr):\n    sub = sc[(sc.Product_ID==p)&(sc.Producer==pr)]\n    res=[]\n    for tau, bg in sub.groupby('Supply_Day'):\n        num=0.0; den=0.0\n        for r_ in bg.itertuples():\n            f = r_.Qty/lev[(r_.Store_ID,p)]\n            d = r_.Day\n            if d+7>365: continue\n            rv = R[(r_.Store_ID,p)].loc[d+1:d+7].values\n            if np.isfinite(rv).all():\n                num += f*(g*rv).sum(); den += f*f*gg\n        if den>0:\n            res.append((tau, -num/den, den, int(bg.Qty.sum())))\n    return pd.DataFrame(res, columns=['supply_day','rho_hat','weight','units'])\n\nfor (p,pr) in [('P_13','PR_3'),('P_8','PR_28'),('P_24','PR_38'),('P_21','PR_14')]:\n    be = batch_effects(p,pr)\n    w = be.weight; r = be.rho_hat\n    print(f'== {p} {pr}: {len(be)} batches, total units {be.units.sum()}')\n    print('  rho_hat percentiles:', np.round(np.percentile(r,[5,25,50,75,95]),2))\n    # weighted histogram\n    hist,edges = np.histogram(r, bins=[-2,-0.5,-0.2,0.2,0.5,0.8,1.1,2], weights=None)\n    print('  counts in bins [-2,-0.5,-0.2,0.2,0.5,0.8,1.1,2]:', hist)\n    big = be[be.weight>0.05]\n    hist2,_ = np.histogram(big.rho_hat, bins=[-2,-0.5,-0.2,0.2,0.5,0.8,1.1,2])\n    print('  well-measured batches (weight>0.05):', len(big), 'bins:', hist2)\nEOF","description":"Per-batch effect distribution for strong pairs","timeout":180000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8193,"cache_read_input_tokens":96531,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":8193,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":30,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"40279f58-a248-4aee-8754-732022a1e743","request_id":"req_011CbwP85FwNqyiruGZANvGf"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_0142UwD1WpPeVGTKk3Ki7Wpe","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01KTS7EoHYyeKZPJ4gYrMyT4","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom numpy.linalg import solve, inv\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\nresid={}; lev={}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col]/cal[col[1]]; l=base.median(); lev[col]=l; resid[col]=base/l-1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\n\n# ---- AR whitening filter from pooled ACF\nacf = [1.0, 0.397, 0.296, 0.196, 0.162, 0.105, 0.055, 0.021]\nimport scipy.linalg as sla\np_ar = 7\nRm = sla.toeplitz(acf[:p_ar])\nphi = solve(Rm, np.array(acf[1:p_ar+1]))\nprint('AR coefs:', np.round(phi,3))\n\ndef whiten(x):\n    # y_t = x_t - sum phi_i x_{t-i}\n    out = x.copy().astype(float)\n    for i,ph in enumerate(phi, start=1):\n        out[i:] -= ph*x[:-i]\n    out[:p_ar] = np.nan\n    return out\n\nk = np.array([1.0, 1.04, 1.01, 0.51, 0.45, 0.37, 0.33]+[0.07]*7)\nprods=[f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]\nstores = sorted({c[0] for c in D.columns}, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nPRODUCERS = sorted(sc.Producer.unique(), key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1]))\npidx={pr:i for i,pr in enumerate(PRODUCERS)}\n\nout={}\nfor p in prods:\n    sub = sc[sc.Product_ID==p]\n    Xr=[]; yr=[]\n    for s in stores:\n        ss = sub[sub.Store_ID==s]\n        U = np.zeros((366,60))\n        for r_ in ss.itertuples():\n            U[r_.Day, pidx[r_.Producer]] += r_.Qty\n        U /= lev[(s,p)]\n        G = np.zeros((366,60))\n        for kk,kv in enumerate(k, start=1):\n            G[1+kk:366,:] += kv*U[1:366-kk,:]\n        rvec = R[(s,p)].reindex(range(366)).values\n        # whiten y and each column of G with same filter\n        yw = whiten(rvec[1:])\n        Gw = np.apply_along_axis(whiten, 0, G[1:])\n        # per-series scale\n        sd = np.nanstd(yw)\n        mask = np.isfinite(yw) & np.isfinite(Gw).all(axis=1)\n        Xr.append(Gw[mask]/sd); yr.append(yw[mask]/sd)\n    X=np.vstack(Xr); y=np.concatenate(yr)\n    keep = (X!=0).any(axis=0)\n    Xk=X[:,keep]\n    XtX = Xk.T@Xk + 1e-8*np.eye(Xk.shape[1])\n    beta = solve(XtX, Xk.T@y)\n    res = y-Xk@beta\n    sigma2 = (res*res).sum()/(len(y)-Xk.shape[1])\n    se = np.sqrt(np.diag(inv(XtX))*sigma2)\n    t = beta/se\n    names=[PRODUCERS[i] for i in range(60) if keep[i]]\n    out[p]=pd.DataFrame({'beta':beta,'t':t}, index=names).sort_values('t')\npickle.dump(out, open('/app/work/gls_reg.pkl','wb'))\nallt = np.concatenate([df.t.values for df in out.values()])\nprint('N pairs:', len(allt))\nprint('t percentiles:', np.round(np.percentile(allt,[0.5,1,5,25,50,75,95,99,99.5]),2))\nfor thr in [3,3.5,4,4.5,5,6]:\n    print(f' t<-{thr}: {(allt<-thr).sum()}   t>{thr}: {(allt>thr).sum()}')\nprint('max positive t:', allt.max().round(2))\n# null sigma from central part\ncore = allt[(allt>-3)&(allt<3)]\nprint('central std:', core.std().round(3))\nEOF","description":"GLS-whitened kernel regression with calibrated null","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6106,"cache_read_input_tokens":104724,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":6106,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":63,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"01c094ed-71e8-4c69-951e-4c962c44c1eb","request_id":"req_011CbwPELsxGRvbXpvysyfF2"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_011Xv2WnH9hkBsFeoGLJ4HdV","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01FoKUFfR1BVq5vyyTHQgqBz","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nout = pickle.load(open('/app/work/gls_reg.pkl','rb'))\nold = pickle.load(open('/app/work/kernel_reg.pkl','rb'))\nsh = pd.read_csv('/app/work/split_half.csv').set_index(['product','producer'])\nrows=[]\nfor p,df in out.items():\n    for pr,r in df.iterrows():\n        o = old[p].loc[pr]\n        key=(p,pr)\n        if key in sh.index:\n            s=sh.loc[key]; rows.append((p,pr,r.beta,r.t,o.beta,o.t,s.t_even,s.t_odd))\n        else:\n            rows.append((p,pr,r.beta,r.t,o.beta,o.t,np.nan,np.nan))\nallr = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['prod','producer','b_gls','t_gls','b_ols','t_ols','t_even','t_odd']).sort_values('t_gls')\nsub = allr[allr.t_gls<-2.8]\npd.set_option('display.width',200)\nprint(sub.to_string(index=False))\nprint()\nts = np.sort(allr.t_gls.values)\nneg = ts[ts<-3]\nprint('gaps in sorted negative t:')\ndif = np.diff(neg)\nfor i in np.argsort(dif)[-8:]:\n    print(f'  gap {dif[i]:.2f} between {neg[i]:.2f} and {neg[i+1]:.2f}')\nEOF","description":"Boundary region analysis with split-half cross-reference"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4647,"cache_read_input_tokens":110830,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4647,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":70,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"c1fb6d70-330e-4099-94a1-1bed1b8cdf36","request_id":"req_011CbwPKszcAjUxC3Ck7d2bD"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01LaikDdR6MstLchMhgjkKA6","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01539ujcdrSZy7Tp4ChfcGP7","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\ngls = pickle.load(open('/app/work/gls_reg.pkl','rb'))\nresid={}; lev={}\nfor col in D.columns:\n    base = D[col]/cal[col[1]]; l=base.median(); lev[col]=l; resid[col]=base/l-1.0\nR = pd.DataFrame(resid)\ng = np.array([1.0, 1.04, 1.01, 0.51, 0.45, 0.37, 0.33])\ngg=(g*g).sum()\n\n# per-series robust sd\nsds = {col: 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(R[col]-R[col].median())) for col in R.columns}\n\n# day-level composition\ngrp = sc.groupby(['Store_ID','Product_ID','Day'])\nday_prods = grp.Producer.nunique()\nday_qty = grp.Qty.sum()\n\nrows=[]\nfor (s,p,d), npr in day_prods.items():\n    if npr==1 and d+7<=365:\n        sub = sc[(sc.Store_ID==s)&(sc.Product_ID==p)&(sc.Day==d)]\n        pr = sub.Producer.iloc[0]\n        q = sub.Qty.sum()\n        rv = R[(s,p)].loc[d+1:d+7].values\n        if np.isfinite(rv).all():\n            z = (g*rv).sum()/(sds[(s,p)]*np.sqrt(gg))\n            f = q/lev[(s,p)]\n            rows.append((p,pr,s,d,q,round(f,2),round(z,2)))\nsole = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['prod','producer','store','day','qty','f','z'])\nsole.to_csv('/app/work/sole_days.csv', index=False)\nprint('total sole-producer days:', len(sole))\n# join with pair-level t\ntmap = {}\nfor p,df in gls.items():\n    for pr,r in df.iterrows(): tmap[(p,pr)]=r.t\nsole['pair_t'] = [tmap.get((r.prod,r.producer),np.nan) for r in sole.itertuples()]\nbad = sole[sole.pair_t<-4.4]; good = sole[sole.pair_t>-2]\nprint('sole days of bad pairs:', len(bad), ' mean z:', bad.z.mean().round(2), ' frac z<-2.5:', (bad.z<-2.5).mean().round(2))\nprint('sole days of null pairs:', len(good), ' mean z:', good.z.mean().round(2), ' frac z<-2.5:', (good.z<-2.5).mean().round(2))\nprint()\nprint('bad-pair sole days with z<-2.5:')\nprint(bad[bad.z<-2.5].sort_values(['prod','producer']).to_string(index=False))\n# how many bad pairs have at least one sole day at all, and with dip\nbb = bad.groupby(['prod','producer']).agg(n=('z','size'), minz=('z','min'))\nprint()\nprint('per bad pair: sole days count / min z:')\nprint(bb.sort_index().to_string())\nnpairs_with_sole = len(bb); \nprint('bad pairs with >=1 sole day:', npairs_with_sole, 'of', (pd.Series(tmap)< -4.4).sum())\nprint('bad pairs with sole-day z<-2.5:', (bb.minz<-2.5).sum())\nEOF","description":"Sole-producer day dip analysis for all pairs","timeout":180000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6594,"cache_read_input_tokens":115477,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":6594,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":30,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"ba8955c6-e225-413a-af5b-640040379b2c","request_id":"req_011CbwPNRtZ4Lwr6CUrnMyAU"}

np.array([1.0,1.04,1.01,0.51,0.45,0.37,0.33]+[0.07]*7)\nprods=[f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]\nstores = sorted({c[0] for c in D.columns}, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nPRODUCERS = sorted(sc.Producer.unique(), key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1]))\npidx={pr:i for i,pr in enumerate(PRODUCERS)}\n\nNSHIFT=500\nresults=[]\nfor p in prods:\n    cand = [pr for pr,r in gls[p].iterrows() if r.t < -3.0]\n    if not cand: continue\n    sub = sc[sc.Product_ID==p]\n    Xblocks=[]; yblocks=[]; Ublocks=[]\n    for s in stores:\n        ss=sub[sub.Store_ID==s]\n        U=np.zeros((366,60))\n        for r_ in ss.itertuples(): U[r_.Day, pidx[r_.Producer]] += r_.Qty\n        U/=lev[(s,p)]\n        G=np.zeros((366,60))\n        for kk,kv in enumerate(k,start=1): G[1+kk:366,:]+=kv*U[1:366-kk,:]\n        rvec=R[(s,p)].reindex(range(366)).values\n        yw=whiten(rvec[1:]); Gw=np.apply_along_axis(whiten,0,G[1:])\n        sd=np.nanstd(yw)\n        mask=np.isfinite(yw)&np.isfinite(Gw).all(axis=1)\n        Xblocks.append(Gw[mask]/sd); yblocks.append(yw[mask]/sd)\n        Ublocks.append((U[1:][mask[0:0].shape[0]*0:], mask))  # keep U & mask per store\n    X=np.vstack(Xblocks); y=np.concatenate(yblocks)\n    keep=(X!=0).any(axis=0); names=[PRODUCERS[i] for i in range(60) if keep[i]]\n    Xk=X[:,keep]\n    nidx={nm:i for i,nm in Xk[:,j]\n        xt = xobs - Q@(Q.T@xobs)\n        beta = xt@ytil/(xt@xt)\n        s2 = ((ytil-beta*xt)**2).sum()/(len(y)-Xk.shape[1])\n        tobs = beta/np.sqrt(s2/(xt@xt))\n        # placebo: circular shifts of focal column per store block\n        tnull=[]\n        lens=[len(b) for b in Xblocks]\n        offs=np.cumsum([0]+lens)\n        for it in range(NSHIFT):\n            xs=np.empty_like(xobs)\n            for bi in range(len(Xblocks)):\n                blk = xobs[offs[bi]:offs[bi+1]]\n                sh = rng.integers(20, len(blk)-20)\n                xs[offs[bi]:offs[bi+1]] = np.roll(blk, sh)\n            xst = xs - Q@(Q.T@xs)\n            den = xst@xst\n            if den<=0: continue\n            b2 = xst@ytil/den\n            tnull.append(b2/np.sqrt(s2/den))\n        tnull=np.array(tnull)\n        zstar=(tobs-tnull.mean())/tnull.std()\n        pemp=(tnull<=tobs).mean()\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01YLPgUv63i5Twz4Z2zfzwRb","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01KJePHRhKMN84ZUAdc2azv9","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom numpy.linalg import solve, inv\nimport scipy.linalg as sla\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); N = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/N.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\n\n# count-channel: normalize N by its own per-(s,p) level and product calendar (re-derive calendar for N)\nprods=[f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]\nstores = sorted({c[0] for c in D.columns}, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nnormN = N / N.median(axis=0)\ncalN = {p: normN[[(s,p) for s in stores]].median(axis=1) for p in prods}\nresidN={}\nfor col in N.columns:\n    base = N[col]/calN[col[1]]; l=base.median(); residN[col]=base/l-1.0\nRN = pd.DataFrame(residN)\n# demand level for exposure scaling (consistent with D channel)\nlevD = {col: (D[col]/cal[col[1]]).median() for col in D.columns}\n\n# ACF of N residuals\nac=[1.0]\nfor lag in range(1,8):\n    num=0.0;den=0.0\n    for col in RN.columns:\n        r=RN[col].values; r=r-np.nanmean(r)\n        num+=np.nansum(r[:-lag]*r[lag:]); den+=np.nansum(r*r)\n    ac.append(num/den)\nprint('N-residual ACF:', np.round(ac,3))\nphi = solve(sla.toeplitz(ac[:7]), np.array(ac[1:8]))\ndef whiten(x):\n    out=x.copy().astype(float)\n    for i,ph in enumerate(phi,start=1): out[i:]-=ph*x[:-i]\n    out[:7]=np.nan\n    return out\nk = np.array([1.0,1.04,1.01,0.51,0.45,0.37,0.33]+[0.07]*7)\nPRODUCERS = sorted(sc.Producer.unique(), key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1]))\npidx={pr:i for i,pr in enumerate(PRODUCERS)}\nout={}\nfor p in prods:\n    sub=sc[sc.Product_ID==p]; Xr=[];yr=[]\n    for s in stores:\n        ss=sub[sub.Store_ID==s]\n        U=np.zeros((366,60))\n        for r_ in ss.itertuples(): U[r_.Day,pidx[r_.Producer]]+=r_.Qty\n        U/=levD[(s,p)]\n        G=np.zeros((366,60))\n        for kk,kv in enumerate(k,start=1): G[1+kk:366,:]+=kv*U[1:366-kk,:]\n        rvec=RN[(s,p)].reindex(range(366)).values\n        yw=whiten(rvec[1:]); Gw=np.apply_along_axis(whiten,0,G[1:])\n        sd=np.nanstd(yw)\n        mask=np.isfinite(yw)&np.isfinite(Gw).all(axis=1)\n        Xr.append(Gw[mask]/sd); yr.append(yw[mask]/sd)\n    X=np.vstack(Xr); y=np.concatenate(yr)\n    keep=(X!=0).any(axis=0); Xk=X[:,keep]\n    XtX=Xk.T@Xk+1e-8*np.eye(Xk.shape[1])\n    beta=solve(XtX,Xk.T@y)\n    s2=((y-Xk@beta)**2).sum()/(len(y)-Xk.shape[1])\n    se=np.sqrt(np.diag(inv(XtX))*s2); t=beta/se\n    names=[PRODUCERS[i] for i in range(60) if keep[i]]\n    out[p]=pd.Series(t,index=names)\npickle.dump(out,open('/app/work/gls_N.pkl','wb'))\nallt=np.concatenate([v.values for v in out.values()])\nprint('N-channel t percentiles:', np.round(np.percentile(allt,[0.5,5,25,50,75,95,99.5]),2))\nprint('t<-4:',(allt<-4).sum(),' t>4:',(allt>4).sum(),' max:',allt.max().round(2))\nEOF","description":"GLS regression on the visit-count channel","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":11790,"cache_read_input_tokens":129269,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":11790,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":53,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"a192330b-e2cf-43cf-8623-338181d8d622","request_id":"req_011CbwPgJAxpqPsbHCCcvuf3"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01N1mu95vuYmDJR2AubsGECM","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_019kdZjAkgT1XQFDkBn1RRTX","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nglsD = pickle.load(open('/app/work/gls_reg.pkl','rb'))\nglsN = pickle.load(open('/app/work/gls_N.pkl','rb'))\nold = pickle.load(open('/app/work/kernel_reg.pkl','rb'))\nrows=[]\nfor p in glsD:\n    for pr,r in glsD[p].iterrows():\n        tN = glsN[p].get(pr, np.nan)\n        rows.append((p,pr,r.beta,r.t,tN,old[p].loc[pr].t))\ndf = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['prod','producer','beta','tD','tN','tOLS'])\n\n# calibrate each channel from central bulk (exclude left tail)\ndef calib(x):\n    c = x[(x>-2.5)]\n    med = np.median(c)\n    sig = (np.percentile(c,97.5)-med)/1.96\n    return med, sig\nmD,sD = calib(df.tD.values); mN,sN = calib(df.tN.values); mO,sO = calib(df.tOLS.values)\nprint('null D: %.2f±%.2f  N: %.2f±%.2f  OLS: %.2f±%.2f'%(mD,sD,mN,sN,mO,sO))\ndf['zD']=(df.tD-mD)/sD; df['zN']=(df.tN-mN)/sN; df['zO']=(df.tOLS-mO)/sO\n# correlation of channels under null\nnul = df[(df.zD>-2.5)&(df.zN>-2.5)]\nrho = np.corrcoef(nul.zD, nul.zN)[0,1]\nprint('null channel correlation D-N:', round(rho,2))\ndf['zC'] = (df.zD+df.zN)/np.sqrt(2+2*rho)\ndf = df.sort_values('zC')\nsub = df[df.zC<-2.5]\npd.set_option('display.width',250); pd.set_option('display.max_rows',300)\nprint(sub.round(2).to_string(index=False))\nprint()\nv=df.zC.values\nfor thr in [3,3.5,4,4.5,5]:\n    print(f'zC<-{thr}: {(v<-thr).sum()}  zC>{thr}: {(v>thr).sum()}')\nprint('max positive zC:', v.max().round(2))\ndf.to_csv('/app/work/combined.csv', index=False)\nEOF","description":"Combine demand and count channels with calibrated nulls"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":9725,"cache_read_input_tokens":141059,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":9725,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":32,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"a4419660-6ac8-4117-857b-6343eecf62b8","request_id":"req_011CbwPsLRTGFbSfqx6FYUma"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01TytfqGzoXyk7nTwEf3D8pG","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0156CHejYy1oNAnqD65yKZw4","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle, json\nfrom numpy.linalg import solve\nimport scipy.linalg as sla\ndf = pd.read_csv('/app/work/combined.csv')\nconf = df[df.zC <= -4.0].copy()\nprint('confirmed pairs:', len(conf))\n\n# collinearity check among confirmed pairs within product\nD = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/D.pkl'); cal = pd.read_pickle('/app/work/cal.pkl')\nsc = pd.read_csv('/app/work/sold_composition.csv')\nlev = {col: (D[col]/cal[col[1]]).median() for col in D.columns}\nacf=[1.0,0.397,0.296,0.196,0.162,0.105,0.055,0.021]\nphi = solve(sla.toeplitz(acf[:7]), np.array(acf[1:8]))\ndef whiten(x):\n    out=x.copy().astype(float)\n    for i,ph in enumerate(phi,start=1): out[i:]-=ph*x[:-i]\n    out[:7]=np.nan; return out\nk = np.array([1.0,1.04,1.01,0.51,0.45,0.37,0.33]+[0.07]*7)\nstores = sorted({c[0] for c in D.columns}, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nworst=[]\nfor p, grp in conf.groupby('prod'):\n    prs = list(grp.producer)\n    if len(prs)<2: continue\n    sub = sc[sc.Product_ID==p]\n    cols={}\n    for pr in prs:\n        blocks=[]\n        for s in stores:\n            ss=sub[(sub.Store_ID==s)&(sub.Producer==pr)]\n            U=np.zeros(366)\n            for r_ in ss.itertuples(): U[r_.Day]+=r_.Qty\n            U/=lev[(s,p)]\n            G=np.zeros(366)\n            for kk,kv in enumerate(k,start=1): G[1+kk:366]+=kv*U[1:366-kk]\n            blocks.append(whiten(G[1:]))\n        cols[pr]=np.concatenate(blocks)\n    for i in range(len(prs)):\n        for j in range(i+1,len(prs)):\n            a,b = cols[prs[i]], cols[prs[j]]\n            m = np.isfinite(a)&np.isfinite(b)\n            c = np.corrcoef(a[m],b[m])[0,1]\n            worst.append((p,prs[i],prs[j],round(c,3)))\nworst.sort(key=lambda x:-abs(x[3]))\nprint('top design correlations among confirmed pairs:', worst[:8])\n\n# build answer\nans = {f'P_{i}': [] for i in range(1,31)}\nfor r in conf.itertuples():\n    ans[r.prod].append(r.producer)\nfor p in ans:\n    ans[p] = sorted(ans[p], key=lambda x:int(x.split('_')[1]))\njson.dump(ans, open('/app/answer.json','w'), indent=1)\nfor p in [f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]:\n    print(p, ans[p])\nprint('total:', sum(len(v) for v in ans.values()))\nEOF","description":"Collinearity check and write {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01QCRt2t1ZYHV61ygjZPMcD8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018zgPJPpbPgXLR77c5vSZG9","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-app/memory/supply-chain-offender-analysis.md","content":"---\nname: supply-chain-offender-analysis\ndescription: Methodology and key findings for the /app supply chain quality-offender task (answer in /app/answer.json, scripts in /app/work/)\nmetadata:\n  type: project\n---\n\nSolved 2026-06-11. The /app dataset (365 days, 10 stores, 30 products, 60 producers) reconstructs exactly: wholesaler = per-product FIFO, suppliers enqueue in daily receipt sequence, stores served in store sequence, same-day; store sales consume FIFO before that day's delivery arrives. All 3,650 TOTAL_DELIVERED_UNITS matched with zero error (see /app/work/parse_and_simulate.py).\n\nKey non-obvious findings:\n- Rejection events are frequent and small (~half of demand-residual variance), so single-event detection is hopeless; the staged 14-day recovery shows up only in aggregate. Empirical kernel at lags 1-14 ≈ (1, 1.04, 1.01, .51, .45, .37, .33, then ~.07) — flatter tail than the spec's 0.8/0.5/0.2 reductions.\n- Residual autocorrelation (0.40 at lag 1) is fully explained by the kernel's own ACF — not drift.\n- Inference: per-product GLS (AR-whitened) regression of demand residuals on kernel-convolved per-producer sold units, run on both the demand-sum channel and the visit-count channel, combined (null-calibrated; max positive combined z over 728 pairs was +2.85). Confirmed = combined z ≤ -4.0 → 83 pairs.\n- Circular-shift placebo tests are invalid here (breaking co-occurrence of producer exposures creates a positive bias); calibrate from the observed positive tail instead.\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":822,"cache_read_input_tokens":168788,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":822,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":56,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"d54765ce-51e4-46a5-abb4-12c621daf7f6","request_id":"req_011CbwQ5zgQqKrp3XV4xcco4"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01NL529uKfemzAJxbX2pWFpo","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Done — the answer is written to `/app/answer.json` with 83 confirmed producer-product pairs across the 30 products.\n\n## How I got there\n\n**1. Exact flow reconstruction.** I parsed all four raw sources (4 file formats each) and simulated the wholesaler as a per-product FIFO: each day, supplier batches enqueue in the `DAILY_SEQUENCE_NO` order from the wholesaler receipts, then store orders are filled in store sequence order (\"brutal first-come\"). This reproduced all 3,650 `TOTAL_DELIVERED_UNITS` values **with zero mismatches**, validating the conventions. Store-side FIFO consumption (sales drawn before the same day's delivery arrives) also reconciled perfectly with the sales log, giving an exact map of which producer's units were sold at every (store, product, day).\n\n**2. Finding the rejection signature.** Individual dips turned out to be undetectable as discrete events: rejections are frequent and small (a few consumers each). Two pieces of evidence settled this — the pooled lag-profile of demand after suspect sales shows the exact staged recovery (flat ≈3-day dip, ≈half-depth days 4-7, near zero days 8-14), and the demand residuals' autocorrelation matches that kernel's own ACF precisely, implying overlapping recoveries make up ~half the residual variance. Sole-producer sale days followed by visible dips barely exceed the noise background, so event-by-event attribution can't be the basis of \"definitely.\"\n\n**3. Statistical attribution.** For each product I regressed demand residuals (level- and calendar-normalized, with the calendar estimated as the cross-store median) on each producer's sold units convolved with the empirical recovery kernel — AR-whitened GLS, all 60 producers jointly so credit can't leak between co-sold batches. I ran this independently on two channels: total reported demand and consumer visit counts, then combined them. Calibration is clean: across all 728 producer-product pairs, the most extreme *positive* combined score is +2.85, while real offenders extend to -18.6. Split-half (odd/even supply-day) replication confirmed the effects are stable rates, not one-off flukes, and design-collinearity among confirmed pairs is low (max 0.34), so no attribution is confounded.\n\n**Confirmed** = combined score ≤ -4.0, far beyond anything the null side produces (expected false inclusions ≈ 0.005 over the whole answer). That yields 83 pairs, 1–5 producers per product — e.g. `P_13: [PR_3]`-class offenders with per-unit rejection rates up to ~20% of buyer demand, down to mild-but-unambiguous offenders around 3%. Pairs with weaker, channel-inconsistent evidence (combined score between -3 and -4) were deliberately excluded as \"suspected but not definite.\""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":271,"cache_read_input_tokens":170375,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":271,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b22d81-3f23-47e5-a1ea-b9f9686e4497","uuid":"0375eedd-d8b2-4796-b6df-888c7e891369","request_id":"req_011CbwQ7f13MRUPL4XgtoaYQ"}
